title_en: 1. Lying upon Allaah ~ and/or His Messenger ~
title_id: 1. Berdusta atas Nama Allah dan/atau Rasul-Nya
content_en:
Allaah ~ informs us that it is not allowed to follow anything without knowledge. He ~ says: J ~~t ~t 2j\ 0~ ~ '-fl, Su ;:J ~ ~ ~_;~ ), ~ / J. ,. / > 7/ / ,/' / / .. " . .u ->> ,;1 1~. i"~ / Jr .,.-:: .r:.:.aH h//~ "'( O.)_,....... ~~_) j.ll l5°' 1J'. ~/ L>_r' ,, './ ~_)'J" "And you will see on the Day of Judgment, those who lie upon Allaah, their faces blackened." [Az-Zumar (39): 60] lbn AI-Jawzee, in his Tafseer said: "Many scholars of lslaam are of the opinion, that lying upon Allaah ~ and His Messenger ~ is disbelief, and that it takes one out of lslaam (i.e. it makes him a difsbeliever). Lying upon the Messenger of Allaah ~ is similarly serious, since indirectly it is lying upon Allaah 1'. The Messenger ~ emphasizing the severity of lying upon himself ~ said: ,,,. f 0 ...- ,:Ill- "" ,; ,,. o 1 .:-..4 ~~ lj. ::/u u :_;::~ ,:; 1 ;::_ ..::.i.15 ? ,; -! u / . • '-:1"" . u "Whoever knowingly lie upon me, then let him occupy his seat in the Fire.'1.541 Many Muslims would tell you that stealing, bribery, killing, adultery etc. are all prohibited (Haraam) acts.

However, when it comes to lying upon Allaah ~ and/or His Messenger ~ they are [54] Bukhaaree & Muslim. Golden Advice Series 04 not even aware that such a sin exists, and so they would indulge in it freely without worry. This complacency among Muslims regarding this sin comes about because of the great ignorance that prevails concerning i) the rights of Allaah ~ and His Messenger ~ and ii) the serious punishment that results from indulging in sin. This complacent attitude, coupled with ignorance, has been the main factors for the majority of the deviations and differences found in lslaam. Various ways in which lying upon Allaah ~ and His Messenger ~ are manifested. a) Describing Allaah without proof from the Qur'aan or authentic Sunnah For example, describing Allaah ~ as being everywhere is a serious misconception of which many Muslims are guilty.

The Qur'aan and authentic Sunnah are both replete with proofs which show that Allaah ~ is above and beyond His creation, and not a part of it, as the statement that 'He, is everywhere' necessitates. The following hadith clearly indicate this. Mu'aawiyah bin AlHakim said: "I had a servant girl who used to tend to my sheep in the area of Mount Uhud, near a place called AI-Jawwaareeyah. One day I came to see them only to find that a wolf had made off with a sheep from her flock. Since, I, like the rest of Adam's descendants, am prone to do regrettable acts, I gave her a terrible slap on her face.

When I came to Allaah's Messenger ~ with the story, he considered it a grave thing for me to have done. I said: 'O Messenger of Allaah ~ couldn't I free her? He replied: Guarding the Tongue "Bring her to me." So I brought her. He then asked her, i1 _;t "Where is Allaah?" And she replied: "Above the sky." Then he asked her: "Who am I?'' She replied : "You are Allaah's Messenger." So he said: "Free her; for she is a believer."l55l It must be noted that one of the duties of the Messenger ~ was to correct any mistakes anyone committed in this religion (Deen). His silence after asking her, "Where is Allaah?'' and she replied "Above the sky", is an indication that her answer is correct.

His affirming that she is a believer, at the end of the hadith, is the direct proof that her answers were correct, otherwise he would be lying, and we seek refuge with Allaah ~ for even thinking that lying is possible from the Messenger ~ - What should be noted, is the fact that the first question he asked her was: Where is Allaah? and not if she believed in Him, [55] Muslim. Golden Advice Series 04 the Most High. Since the question was: 'Where is Allaah?' great importance and attention must be given to the fact that, since he accepted that Allaah ~ is above the skies as the correct answer, that this is direct proof that this is the correct view on the question. Additionally, it establishes the importance of the issue in lslaam, contrary to the belief and attitude of many Muslims, even so-called Shaykhs, who claim that the issue is either trivial or too advanced. After all, this was the first question the Prophet ~ asked of a slave girl to determine if she was a Muslim or not! b) Explaining the Aayaat of the Qur'aan, or the hadith of the Prophet ~ with meanings other than their apparent ones, is also another way of lying upon Allaah ~ - Allaah ~ says: "(Allaah) said: 'O lblees (Shaytaan)!

What prevents you from prostrating yourself to one whom I have created with both My Hands ... "' [Saad (38): 75] In the above Aayah, Allaah ~ is saying that He created Aadam with His Two Hands. Yet some Muslims try to explain "Alla a h's two Hands" to mean His power. Who knows Allaah ~ better, Himself or these people? As Muslims, we have to believe in Allaah ~ as He describes Himself or as His Messenger ~ described Him.

Therefore, if He ~ says he has Two Hands, we must believe that. However, we must also believe, that they are not hands like our: He, the Sublime says: !,,_ ,? ?" ,I,_~\ ~\ ,,~,, ! , -;: ~~ /1~ "'\ . ,, . e:-;:--- yr, _J ~ ,, / ,, ~.,, Guarding the Tongue "There is nothing like Him; and He is the Hearer; the Seer of all" [Ash-Shooraa (42): 11] c) Making Haraam things Halaal This is another form of lying upon Allaah. An example of this is telling Muslims to get out of the Masjid. Since some of our brothers pray according to the way demonstrated by the Prophet ~ (i.e. according to the Sunnah), some administrators order them out of the Masjid just because of that. These arrogant leaders should remember, that amongst the greatest of oppressors, are those who prevent a Muslim from praying in the Masjid of Allaah.

This is because they are making Haraam the most Halaal of deeds, purely out of their unlawful dictatorship. Allaah ~ says: "And who is more unjust than those who forbid that Allaah's Name should be remembered in His Mosques, and strive after its destruction .. .'' [AI-Baqarah (2): 114] d) Making Halaal things Haraam This is also another way in which Muslims lie upon Allaah ~ - An example of this is that Allaah M permits a Muslim man to marry and have up to four wives, at any given time. However, if anyone attaches conditions, and throws doubt on this, he/she is in fact making something that is Halaal, Haraam. This is done by Muslims saying that a man can only marry once, and if the circumstances of necessity present itself, then the law of polygyny (i.e. a man marrying more than one wife) becomes applicable. Golden Advice Series 04 Allaah, the All-Knowing says: " ... then marry (other) women of your choice, two or three or four, but if you shall not be able to deal justly (with them) then many only one, (or the captives) that your right hands possess ...

" [An-Nisaa' (4): 3] In the aboveAayah, Allaah ~ , the Wise has legislated that a man may marry 2, 3 or 4 wives, but if he was afraid of treating them unjustly, that is, he would not be able to keep up a balanced scale of equitable treatment, then the alternative is to marry only one. This, and only this, is the condition set down by the Sharee'ah. Anyone trying to say that there are other conditions is inventing his own law. Those who propagate that the first wife must be barren or sick, or that she has to approve of the second marriage, etc., have no evidence at all to support their false claims. Even worst, are those who have adopted the orientalists' opinion, that polygyny is a lustful act.

Thus, anyone whose wife is otherwise perfect, but he still takes a second wife, is, in the orientalists' view, doing this only out of evil lust and desires. This category is worst, because their statement is blasphemy against the Qur'aan, since Allaah ~ , the Wise, promotes and encourages therein, that a man can be married to more than one woman at any given time. It is also blasphemy to the Prophet ~ , his Companions, and the scholars of lslaam, as they practiced it during their lifetime. However, even if it were done purely for lust or desire, it would still be according to the Sunnah, since the Messenger of Allaah Guarding the Tongue ~ informs us, that when someone satisfies his sexual cravings or lust in a Ha/oaf way, he will be blessed for it. He states: ..,.,,,. ,,,.

' 't.- 0: ~J..p r-5~1 ~ Jj / " ... and even when one of you cohabits (engage in sexual intercourse), he is rewarded for it." We (the Companions) said: "O Messenger of Allaah ~ ! Would one of us earn blessings and rewards for satisfying his (sexual) lusts and desires?" He said: ~).,, )~ a I'.: ~ ~ ~tsf ~ I', . 1-:: ::: ';. ' •\ •:•f,f -: ~ '" i ? c..1,- ~ _., .,., r ) , " .,, . i j ~l5 ~Jy{;j1 . 1-:: ::: ';. ' 111 dlK; _r:- , ~ ~.) ~ / " / '·Yes, don't you see that if he was to satisfy it in the Haraam way (by adultery and fornication) that he would be sinned? Similarly, when he satisfies it in the Ha/oaf way, he is rewarded."f.561 e) Using fabricated and unauthentic Ahaadeeth of the Messenger of Allaah ~ is another way of lying on Allaah and His Messenger ~ - The Messenger of Allaah ~ said: 81 : }j~ i.,::,t; 1J:..;::~ ~ \.:'..~.is -~.; --d ~ • " '-!""' (._/ "Whoever knowingly lie upon me, then let him occupy his seat in the Fire (of Hell).''f.571 [56) Muslim. [57) Bukhaaree & Muslim. Golden Advice Series 04 The Messenger of Allaah ~ also said: "Whoever relates a hadith about me, knowing it to be inauthentic, is himself a liar."l58l
content_id:
1. Berdusta atas Nama Allah dan/atau Rasul-Nya

Allah memberitahu kita bahwa tidak diperbolehkan mengikuti sesuatu tanpa ilmu. Allah berfirman: "Dan kamu akan melihat pada hari kiamat, orang-orang yang berdusta atas nama Allah, wajah mereka menghitam." (Az-Zumar [39]: 60)

Ibn al-Jawzi rahimahullah dalam tafsirnya berkata: "Banyak ulama Islam berpendapat bahwa berdusta atas nama Allah dan Rasul-Nya adalah kekufuran, dan bahwa hal itu mengeluarkan seseorang dari Islam. Berdusta atas nama Rasulullah ﷺ sama seriusnya, karena secara tidak langsung itu adalah berdusta atas nama Allah."

Rasulullah ﷺ menegaskan keseriusan berdusta atas namanya dengan sabdanya: "Barang siapa yang berdusta atas namaku dengan sengaja, maka hendaklah ia mengambil tempat duduknya di neraka." (HR. Bukhari dan Muslim)

Banyak Muslim yang mengatakan kepada Anda bahwa mencuri, suap, membunuh, zina, dan sebagainya adalah perbuatan haram. Namun, dalam hal berdusta atas nama Allah dan/atau Rasul-Nya, mereka bahkan tidak menyadari bahwa dosa semacam itu ada, sehingga mereka melakukannya dengan bebas tanpa kekhawatiran. Sikap masa bodoh di kalangan Muslim terhadap dosa ini muncul karena kebodohan yang sangat besar yang berlaku mengenai: (i) hak-hak Allah dan Rasul-Nya, dan (ii) hukuman berat yang diakibatkan oleh perbuatan dosa. Sikap masa bodoh yang dibarengi dengan kebodohan ini telah menjadi faktor utama bagi sebagian besar penyimpangan dan perselisihan yang terdapat dalam Islam.

Berbagai cara berdusta atas nama Allah dan Rasul-Nya:

a) Mendeskripsikan Allah tanpa dalil dari Al-Quran atau Sunnah yang sahih

Misalnya, mendeskripsikan Allah sebagai ada di mana-mana adalah kesalahpahaman serius yang dilakukan oleh banyak Muslim. Al-Quran dan Sunnah yang sahih keduanya penuh dengan bukti yang menunjukkan bahwa Allah berada di atas dan melampaui ciptaan-Nya, dan bukan bagian darinya, sebagaimana pernyataan bahwa "Dia ada di mana-mana" mengharuskan.

Mu'awiyah bin al-Hakim berkata: "Aku memiliki seorang budak perempuan yang biasa menggembalakan dombaku di daerah Gunung Uhud, dekat tempat yang disebut al-Jawwariyyah. Suatu hari aku datang untuk melihat mereka, hanya untuk mendapati bahwa seekor serigala telah membawa seekor domba dari kawanannya. Karena aku, seperti keturunan Adam lainnya, cenderung melakukan tindakan yang menyesal, aku memberinya tamparan keras di wajahnya. Ketika aku datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dengan cerita itu, beliau menganggapnya sebagai hal yang serius bagiku. Aku berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah ﷺ, apakah aku tidak bisa membebaskannya?' Beliau menjawab: 'Bawalah dia kepadaku.' Maka aku membawanya. Beliau kemudian bertanya kepadanya: 'Di mana Allah?' Dan dia menjawab: 'Di atas langit.' Kemudian beliau bertanya: 'Siapakah aku?' Dia menjawab: 'Engkau adalah Rasulullah.' Maka beliau bersabda: 'Bebaskan dia; karena dia adalah seorang mukminah.'" (HR. Muslim)

Perlu dicatat bahwa salah satu tugas Rasulullah ﷺ adalah meluruskan kesalahan apapun yang dilakukan seseorang dalam agama ini. Diamnya beliau setelah bertanya kepada wanita itu, "Di mana Allah?" dan dia menjawab "Di atas langit", merupakan indikasi bahwa jawabannya benar. Penegasannya bahwa dia adalah seorang mukminah di akhir hadis adalah bukti langsung bahwa jawabannya benar.

b) Menafsirkan ayat-ayat Al-Quran, atau hadis Nabi ﷺ dengan makna selain makna zahirnya

Allah berfirman: "(Allah) berfirman: 'Hai Iblis (Syaitan)! Apa yang menghalangimu untuk bersujud kepada (Adam) yang telah Kuciptakan dengan kedua tangan-Ku...'" (Shad [38]: 75)

Dalam ayat ini, Allah menyatakan bahwa Dia menciptakan Adam dengan Kedua Tangan-Nya. Namun sebagian Muslim mencoba menafsirkan "Dua Tangan Allah" sebagai kekuasaan-Nya. Siapa yang lebih mengenal Allah, Dia sendiri atau orang-orang ini? Sebagai Muslim, kita harus beriman kepada Allah sebagaimana Dia mendeskripsikan diri-Nya atau sebagaimana Rasul-Nya ﷺ mendeskripsikan-Nya. Oleh karena itu, jika Dia berfirman bahwa Dia memiliki Dua Tangan, kita harus mempercayainya. Namun kita juga harus meyakini bahwa kedua tangan itu tidak seperti tangan kita. Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman: "Tidak ada sesuatupun yang serupa dengan Dia, dan Dia-lah yang Maha Mendengar dan Maha Melihat." (Asy-Syura [42]: 11)

c) Menjadikan hal yang haram sebagai halal

Ini adalah bentuk lain dari berdusta atas nama Allah. Contohnya adalah memerintahkan Muslim untuk keluar dari masjid. Karena sebagian saudara kita shalat sesuai cara yang dicontohkan oleh Nabi ﷺ (yaitu sesuai Sunnah), beberapa pengurus memerintahkan mereka keluar dari masjid hanya karena alasan itu. Para pemimpin yang sombong ini harus ingat, bahwa termasuk orang-orang yang paling zalim adalah mereka yang menghalangi seorang Muslim dari shalat di masjid Allah. Allah berfirman: "Dan siapakah yang lebih zalim daripada orang yang melarang menyebut nama Allah dalam masjid-masjid-Nya, dan berusaha untuk merobohkannya..." (Al-Baqarah [2]: 114)

d) Menjadikan hal yang halal sebagai haram

Ini juga merupakan cara lain Muslim berdusta atas nama Allah. Contohnya adalah bahwa Allah memperbolehkan seorang laki-laki Muslim untuk menikah hingga empat istri sekaligus. Namun, jika seseorang memberikan syarat-syarat tambahan dan meragukan hal ini, ia pada dasarnya menjadikan sesuatu yang halal menjadi haram.

Allah Yang Maha Mengetahui berfirman: "...maka kawinilah wanita-wanita (lain) yang kamu senangi: dua, tiga atau empat. Kemudian jika kamu takut tidak akan dapat berlaku adil, maka (kawinilah) seorang saja..." (An-Nisa' [4]: 3)

Dalam ayat ini, Allah Yang Maha Bijaksana menetapkan bahwa seorang laki-laki boleh menikah dengan 2, 3, atau 4 istri, tetapi jika ia khawatir tidak dapat berlaku adil, maka alternatifnya adalah menikah hanya dengan satu. Inilah satu-satunya syarat yang ditetapkan oleh syariat. Siapapun yang mencoba mengatakan bahwa ada syarat-syarat lain berarti ia membuat hukumnya sendiri. Mereka yang mempropagandakan bahwa istri pertama harus mandul atau sakit, atau bahwa ia harus menyetujui pernikahan kedua, dan sebagainya, sama sekali tidak memiliki dalil untuk mendukung klaim mereka yang batil.

e) Menggunakan hadis-hadis palsu dan tidak sahih dari Rasulullah ﷺ

Ini adalah cara lain berdusta atas nama Allah dan Rasul-Nya. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barang siapa yang berdusta atas namaku dengan sengaja, maka hendaklah ia mengambil tempat duduknya di neraka." (HR. Bukhari dan Muslim)

Rasulullah ﷺ juga bersabda: "Barang siapa yang meriwayatkan suatu hadis tentang aku, mengetahui bahwa hadis itu tidak sahih, maka dia sendiri adalah seorang pendusta."
